net.PSA_92_14: They bear fruit even when they are old; they are filled with vitality and have many leaves.
net.PSA_92_15: So they proclaim that the Lord, my Protector, is just and never unfair.
net.PSA_93_1: The Lord reigns. He is robed in majesty. The Lord is robed; he wears strength around his waist. Indeed, the world is established; it cannot be moved.
net.PSA_93_2: Your throne has been secure from ancient times; you have always been king.
net.PSA_93_3: The waves roar, O Lord, the waves roar, the waves roar and crash.
net.PSA_93_4: Above the sound of the surging water, and the mighty waves of the sea, the Lord sits enthroned in majesty.
net.PSA_93_5: The rules you set down are completely reliable. Holiness aptly adorns your house, O Lord, forever.
net.PSA_94_1: O Lord, the God who avenges! O God who avenges, reveal your splendor.
net.PSA_94_2: Rise up, O judge of the earth. Pay back the proud.
net.PSA_94_3: O Lord, how long will the wicked, how long will the wicked celebrate?
net.PSA_94_4: They spew out threats and speak defiantly; all the evildoers boast.
net.PSA_94_5: O Lord, they crush your people; they oppress the nation that belongs to you.
net.PSA_94_6: They kill the widow and the resident foreigner, and they murder the fatherless.
net.PSA_94_7: Then they say, “The Lord does not see this; the God of Jacob does not take notice of it.”
net.PSA_94_8: Take notice of this, you ignorant people. You fools, when will you ever understand?
net.PSA_94_9: Does the one who makes the human ear not hear? Does the one who forms the human eye not see?
net.PSA_94_10: Does the one who disciplines the nations not punish? He is the one who imparts knowledge to human beings!
net.PSA_94_11: The Lord knows that peoples’ thoughts are morally bankrupt.
net.PSA_94_12: How blessed is the one whom you instruct, O Lord, the one whom you teach from your law,
net.PSA_94_13: in order to protect him from times of trouble, until the wicked are destroyed.
net.PSA_94_14: Certainly the Lord does not forsake his people; he does not abandon the nation that belongs to him.
net.PSA_94_15: For justice will prevail, and all the morally upright will be vindicated.
net.PSA_94_16: Who will rise up to defend me against the wicked? Who will stand up for me against the evildoers?
net.PSA_94_17: If the Lord had not helped me, I would soon have dwelt in the silence of death.
net.PSA_94_18: If I say, “My foot is slipping,” your loyal love, O Lord, supports me.
net.PSA_94_19: When worries threaten to overwhelm me, your soothing touch makes me happy.
net.PSA_94_20: Cruel rulers are not your allies, those who make oppressive laws.
net.PSA_94_21: They conspire against the blameless, and condemn to death the innocent.
net.PSA_94_22: But the Lord will protect me, and my God will shelter me.
net.PSA_94_23: He will pay them back for their sin. He will destroy them because of their evil; the Lord our God will destroy them.
net.PSA_95_1: Come, let us sing for joy to the Lord. Let us shout out praises to our Protector who delivers us.
net.PSA_95_2: Let us enter his presence with thanksgiving. Let us shout out to him in celebration.
net.PSA_95_3: For the Lord is a great God, a great king who is superior to all gods.
net.PSA_95_4: The depths of the earth are in his hand, and the mountain peaks belong to him.
net.PSA_95_5: The sea is his, for he made it. His hands formed the dry land.
net.PSA_95_6: Come, let us bow down and worship. Let us kneel before the Lord, our Creator.
net.PSA_95_7: For he is our God; we are the people of his pasture, the sheep he owns. Today, if only you would obey him.
net.PSA_95_8: He says, “Do not be stubborn like they were at Meribah, like they were that day at Massah in the wilderness,
net.PSA_95_9: where your ancestors challenged my authority, and tried my patience, even though they had seen my work.
net.PSA_95_10: For forty years I was continually disgusted with that generation, and I said, ‘These people desire to go astray; they do not obey my commands.’
net.PSA_95_11: So I made a vow in my anger, ‘They will never enter into the resting place I had set aside for them.’”
net.PSA_96_1: Sing to the Lord a new song. Sing to the Lord, all the earth.
net.PSA_96_2: Sing to the Lord. Praise his name. Announce every day how he delivers.
net.PSA_96_3: Tell the nations about his splendor. Tell all the nations about his amazing deeds.
net.PSA_96_4: For the Lord is great and certainly worthy of praise; he is more awesome than all gods.
net.PSA_96_5: For all the gods of the nations are worthless, but the Lord made the sky.
net.PSA_96_6: Majestic splendor emanates from him; his sanctuary is firmly established and beautiful.
net.PSA_96_7: Ascribe to the Lord, O families of the nations, ascribe to the Lord splendor and strength.
net.PSA_96_8: Ascribe to the Lord the splendor he deserves. Bring an offering and enter his courts.
net.PSA_96_9: Worship the Lord in holy attire. Tremble before him, all the earth.
net.PSA_96_10: Say among the nations, “The Lord reigns! The world is established; it cannot be moved. He judges the nations fairly.”
net.PSA_96_11: Let the sky rejoice, and the earth be happy. Let the sea and everything in it shout.
net.PSA_96_12: Let the fields and everything in them celebrate. Then let the trees of the forest shout with joy
net.PSA_96_13: before the Lord, for he comes. For he comes to judge the earth. He judges the world fairly, and the nations in accordance with his justice.
net.PSA_97_1: The Lord reigns. Let the earth be happy. Let the many coastlands rejoice.
net.PSA_97_2: Dark clouds surround him; equity and justice are the foundation of his throne.
net.PSA_97_3: Fire goes before him; on every side it burns up his enemies.
net.PSA_97_4: His lightning bolts light up the world; the earth sees and trembles.
net.PSA_97_5: The mountains melt like wax before the Lord, before the Lord of the whole earth.
net.PSA_97_6: The sky declares his justice, and all the nations see his splendor.
net.PSA_97_7: All who worship idols are ashamed, those who boast about worthless idols. All the gods bow down before him.
net.PSA_97_8: Zion hears and rejoices, the towns of Judah are happy, because of your judgments, O Lord.
net.PSA_97_9: For you, O Lord, are the Most High over the whole earth; you are elevated high above all gods.
net.PSA_97_10: You who love the Lord, hate evil! He protects the lives of his faithful followers; he delivers them from the power of the wicked.
net.PSA_97_11: The godly bask in the light; the morally upright experience joy.
net.PSA_97_12: You godly ones, rejoice in the Lord. Give thanks to his holy name.
net.PSA_98_1: Sing to the Lord a new song, for he performs amazing deeds. His right hand and his mighty arm accomplish deliverance.
net.PSA_98_2: The Lord demonstrates his power to deliver; in the sight of the nations he reveals his justice.
net.PSA_98_3: He remains loyal and faithful to the family of Israel. All the ends of the earth see our God deliver us.
net.PSA_98_4: Shout out praises to the Lord, all the earth. Break out in a joyful shout and sing!
net.PSA_98_5: Sing to the Lord accompanied by a harp, accompanied by a harp and the sound of music.
net.PSA_98_6: With trumpets and the blaring of the ram’s horn, shout out praises before the king, the Lord.
net.PSA_98_7: Let the sea and everything in it shout, along with the world and those who live in it.
net.PSA_98_8: Let the rivers clap their hands! Let the mountains sing in unison
net.PSA_98_9: before the Lord. For he comes to judge the earth. He judges the world fairly, and the nations in a just manner.
net.PSA_99_1: The Lord reigns! The nations tremble. He sits enthroned above the cherubim; the earth shakes.
net.PSA_99_2: The Lord is elevated in Zion; he is exalted over all the nations.
net.PSA_99_3: Let them praise your great and awesome name. He is holy!
net.PSA_99_4: The king is strong; he loves justice. You ensure that legal decisions will be made fairly; you promote justice and equity in Jacob.
net.PSA_99_5: Praise the Lord our God. Worship before his footstool. He is holy!
net.PSA_99_6: Moses and Aaron were among his priests; Samuel was one of those who prayed to him. They prayed to the Lord and he answered them.
net.PSA_99_7: He spoke to them from a pillar of cloud; they obeyed his regulations and the ordinance he gave them.
net.PSA_99_8: O Lord our God, you answered them. They found you to be a forgiving God, but also one who punished their sinful deeds.
net.PSA_99_9: Praise the Lord our God! Worship on his holy hill, for the Lord our God is holy.
net.PSA_100_1: Shout out praises to the Lord, all the earth!
net.PSA_100_2: Worship the Lord with joy. Enter his presence with joyful singing.
net.PSA_100_3: Acknowledge that the Lord is God. He made us and we belong to him, we are his people, the sheep of his pasture.
net.PSA_100_4: Enter his gates with thanksgiving, and his courts with praise. Give him thanks. Praise his name.
net.PSA_100_5: For the Lord is good. His loyal love endures, and he is faithful through all generations.
net.PSA_101_1: I will sing about loyalty and justice. To you, O Lord, I will sing praises.
net.PSA_101_2: I will walk in the way of integrity. When will you come to me? I will conduct my business with integrity in the midst of my palace.
net.PSA_101_3: I will not even consider doing what is dishonest. I hate doing evil; I will have no part of it.
net.PSA_101_4: I will have nothing to do with a perverse person; I will not permit evil.
net.PSA_101_5: I will destroy anyone who slanders his neighbor in secret. I will not tolerate anyone who has a haughty demeanor and an arrogant attitude.
net.PSA_101_6: I will favor the honest people of the land, and allow them to live with me. Those who walk in the way of integrity will attend me.
net.PSA_101_7: Deceitful people will not live in my palace. Liars will not be welcome in my presence.
net.PSA_101_8: Each morning I will destroy all the wicked people in the land, and remove all evildoers from the city of the Lord.
net.PSA_102_1: O Lord, hear my prayer. Pay attention to my cry for help.
net.PSA_102_2: Do not ignore me in my time of trouble. Listen to me. When I call out to you, quickly answer me.
net.PSA_102_3: For my days go up in smoke, and my bones are charred as in a fireplace.
net.PSA_102_4: My heart is parched and withered like grass, for I am unable to eat food.
net.PSA_102_5: Because of the anxiety that makes me groan, my bones protrude from my skin.
net.PSA_102_6: I am like an owl in the wilderness; I am like a screech owl among the ruins.
net.PSA_102_7: I stay awake; I am like a solitary bird on a roof.
net.PSA_102_8: All day long my enemies taunt me; those who mock me use my name in their curses.
net.PSA_102_9: For I eat ashes as if they were bread, and mix my drink with my tears,
net.PSA_102_10: because of your anger and raging fury. Indeed, you pick me up and throw me away.
net.PSA_102_11: My days are coming to an end, and I am withered like grass.
net.PSA_102_12: But you, O Lord, rule forever, and your reputation endures.
net.PSA_102_13: You will rise up and have compassion on Zion. For it is time to have mercy on her, for the appointed time has come.
net.PSA_102_14: Indeed, your servants take delight in her stones, and feel compassion for the dust of her ruins.
net.PSA_102_15: The nations will respect the reputation of the Lord, and all the kings of the earth will respect his splendor,
net.PSA_102_16: when the Lord rebuilds Zion, and reveals his splendor,
net.PSA_102_17: when he responds to the prayer of the destitute, and does not reject their request.
net.PSA_102_18: The account of his intervention will be recorded for future generations; people yet to be born will praise the Lord.
net.PSA_102_19: For he will look down from his sanctuary above; from heaven the Lord will look toward earth,
net.PSA_102_20: in order to hear the painful cries of the prisoners, and to set free those condemned to die,
net.PSA_102_21: so they may proclaim the name of the Lord in Zion, and praise him in Jerusalem,
net.PSA_102_22: when the nations gather together, and the kingdoms pay tribute to the Lord.
net.PSA_102_23: He has taken away my strength in the middle of life; he has cut short my days.
net.PSA_102_24: I say, “O my God, please do not take me away in the middle of my life. You endure through all generations.
net.PSA_102_25: In earlier times you established the earth; the skies are your handiwork.
net.PSA_102_26: They will perish, but you will endure. They will wear out like a garment; like clothes you will remove them and they will disappear.
net.PSA_102_27: But you remain; your years do not come to an end.
net.PSA_102_28: The children of your servants will settle down here, and their descendants will live securely in your presence.”
net.PSA_103_1: Praise the Lord, O my soul. With all that is within me, praise his holy name.
net.PSA_103_2: Praise the Lord, O my soul. Do not forget all his kind deeds.
net.PSA_103_3: He is the one who forgives all your sins, who heals all your diseases,
net.PSA_103_4: who delivers your life from the Pit, who crowns you with his loyal love and compassion,
net.PSA_103_5: who satisfies your life with good things, so your youth is renewed like an eagle’s.
net.PSA_103_6: The Lord does what is fair, and executes justice for all the oppressed.
net.PSA_103_7: The Lord revealed his faithful acts to Moses, his deeds to the Israelites.
net.PSA_103_8: The Lord is compassionate and merciful; he is patient and demonstrates great loyal love.
net.PSA_103_9: He does not always accuse, and does not stay angry.
net.PSA_103_10: He does not deal with us as our sins deserve; he does not repay us as our misdeeds deserve.
net.PSA_103_11: For as the skies are high above the earth, so his loyal love towers over his faithful followers.
net.PSA_103_12: As far as the eastern horizon is from the west, so he removes the guilt of our rebellious actions from us.
net.PSA_103_13: As a father has compassion on his children, so the Lord has compassion on his faithful followers.
net.PSA_103_14: For he knows what we are made of; he realizes we are made of clay.
net.PSA_103_15: A person’s life is like grass. Like a flower in the field it flourishes,
net.PSA_103_16: but when the hot wind blows, it disappears, and one can no longer even spot the place where it once grew.
net.PSA_103_17: But the Lord continually shows loyal love to his faithful followers, and is faithful to their descendants,
net.PSA_103_18: to those who keep his covenant, who are careful to obey his commands.
net.PSA_103_19: The Lord has established his throne in heaven; his kingdom extends over everything.
net.PSA_103_20: Praise the Lord, you angels of his, you powerful warriors who carry out his decrees and obey his orders.
net.PSA_103_21: Praise the Lord, all you warriors of his, you servants of his who carry out his desires.
net.PSA_103_22: Praise the Lord, all that he has made, in all the regions of his kingdom. Praise the Lord, O my soul.
net.PSA_104_1: Praise the Lord, O my soul! O Lord my God, you are magnificent. You are robed in splendor and majesty.
net.PSA_104_2: He covers himself with light as if it were a garment. He stretches out the skies like a tent curtain,
net.PSA_104_3: and lays the beams of the upper rooms of his palace on the rain clouds. He makes the clouds his chariot, and travels on the wings of the wind.
net.PSA_104_4: He makes the winds his messengers, and the flaming fire his attendant.
net.PSA_104_5: He established the earth on its foundations; it will never be moved.
net.PSA_104_6: The watery deep covered it like a garment; the waters reached above the mountains.
net.PSA_104_7: Your shout made the waters retreat; at the sound of your thunderous voice they hurried off—
net.PSA_104_8: as the mountains rose up, and the valleys went down— to the place you appointed for them.
net.PSA_104_9: You set up a boundary for them that they could not cross, so that they would not cover the earth again.
net.PSA_104_10: He turns springs into streams; they flow between the mountains.
net.PSA_104_11: They provide water for all the animals in the field; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
net.PSA_104_12: The birds of the sky live beside them; they chirp among the bushes.
net.PSA_104_13: He waters the mountains from the upper rooms of his palace; the earth is full of the fruit you cause to grow.
net.PSA_104_14: He provides grass for the cattle, and crops for people to cultivate, so they can produce food from the ground,
net.PSA_104_15: as well as wine that makes people glad, and olive oil to make their faces shine, as well as bread that sustains them.
net.PSA_104_16: The trees of the Lord receive all the rain they need, the cedars of Lebanon that he planted,
net.PSA_104_17: where the birds make nests, near the evergreens in which the herons live.
net.PSA_104_18: The wild goats live in the high mountains; the rock badgers find safety in the cliffs.
net.PSA_104_19: He made the moon to mark the months, and the sun sets according to a regular schedule.
net.PSA_104_20: You make it dark and night comes, during which all the beasts of the forest prowl around.
net.PSA_104_21: The lions roar for prey, seeking their food from God.
net.PSA_104_22: When the sun rises, they withdraw and sleep in their dens.
net.PSA_104_23: People then go out to do their work, and they labor until evening.
net.PSA_104_24: How many living things you have made, O Lord! You have exhibited great skill in making all of them; the earth is full of the living things you have made.
net.PSA_104_25: Over here is the deep, wide sea, which teems with innumerable swimming creatures, living things both small and large.
net.PSA_104_26: The ships travel there, and over here swims the whale you made to play in it.
net.PSA_104_27: All your creatures wait for you to provide them with food on a regular basis.
net.PSA_104_28: You give food to them and they receive it; you open your hand and they are filled with food.
net.PSA_104_29: When you ignore them, they panic. When you take away their life’s breath, they die and return to dust.
net.PSA_104_30: When you send your life-giving breath, they are created, and you replenish the surface of the ground.
net.PSA_104_31: May the splendor of the Lord endure. May the Lord find pleasure in the living things he has made.
net.PSA_104_32: He looks down on the earth and it shakes; he touches the mountains and they start to smolder.
net.PSA_104_33: I will sing to the Lord as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God as long as I exist.
net.PSA_104_34: May my thoughts be pleasing to him. I will rejoice in the Lord.
net.PSA_104_35: May sinners disappear from the earth, and the wicked vanish. Praise the Lord, O my soul. Praise the Lord.
net.PSA_105_1: Give thanks to the Lord. Call on his name. Make known his accomplishments among the nations.
net.PSA_105_2: Sing to him. Make music to him. Tell about all his miraculous deeds.
net.PSA_105_3: Boast about his holy name. Let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
net.PSA_105_4: Seek the Lord and the strength he gives. Seek his presence continually.
net.PSA_105_5: Recall the miraculous deeds he performed, his mighty acts and the judgments he decreed,
net.PSA_105_6: O children of Abraham, God’s servant, you descendants of Jacob, God’s chosen ones.
net.PSA_105_7: He is the Lord our God; he carries out judgment throughout the earth.
net.PSA_105_8: He always remembers his covenantal decree, the promise he made to a thousand generations—
net.PSA_105_9: the promise he made to Abraham, the promise he made by oath to Isaac.
net.PSA_105_10: He gave it to Jacob as a decree, to Israel as a lasting promise,
net.PSA_105_11: saying, “To you I will give the land of Canaan as the portion of your inheritance.”
net.PSA_105_12: When they were few in number, just a very few, and resident foreigners within it,
net.PSA_105_13: they wandered from nation to nation, and from one kingdom to another.
net.PSA_105_14: He let no one oppress them; he disciplined kings for their sake,
net.PSA_105_15: saying, “Don’t touch my chosen ones. Don’t harm my prophets.”
net.PSA_105_16: He called down a famine upon the earth; he cut off all the food supply.
net.PSA_105_17: He sent a man ahead of them — Joseph was sold as a servant.
net.PSA_105_18: The shackles hurt his feet; his neck was placed in an iron collar,
net.PSA_105_19: until the time when his prediction came true. The Lord’s word proved him right.
net.PSA_105_20: The king authorized his release; the ruler of nations set him free.
net.PSA_105_21: He put him in charge of his palace, and made him manager of all his property,
net.PSA_105_22: giving him authority to imprison his officials and to teach his advisers.
net.PSA_105_23: Israel moved to Egypt; Jacob lived for a time in the land of Ham.
net.PSA_105_24: The Lord made his people very fruitful, and made them more numerous than their enemies.
net.PSA_105_25: He caused the Egyptians to hate his people, and to mistreat his servants.
net.PSA_105_26: He sent his servant Moses, and Aaron, whom he had chosen.
net.PSA_105_27: They executed his miraculous signs among them, and his amazing deeds in the land of Ham.
net.PSA_105_28: He made it dark; Moses and Aaron did not disobey his orders.
net.PSA_105_29: He turned the Egyptians’ water into blood, and killed their fish.
net.PSA_105_30: Their land was overrun by frogs, which even got into the rooms of their kings.
net.PSA_105_31: He ordered flies to come; gnats invaded their whole territory.
net.PSA_105_32: He sent hail along with the rain; there was lightning in their land.
net.PSA_105_33: He destroyed their vines and fig trees, and broke the trees throughout their territory.
net.PSA_105_34: He ordered locusts to come, innumerable grasshoppers.
net.PSA_105_35: They ate all the vegetation in their land, and devoured the crops of their fields.
net.PSA_105_36: He struck down all the firstborn in their land, the firstfruits of their reproductive power.
net.PSA_105_37: He brought his people out enriched with silver and gold; none of his tribes stumbled.
net.PSA_105_38: Egypt was happy when they left, for they were afraid of them.
net.PSA_105_39: He spread out a cloud for a cover, and provided a fire to light up the night.
net.PSA_105_40: They asked for food, and he sent quail; he satisfied them with food from the sky.
net.PSA_105_41: He opened up a rock and water flowed out; a river ran through dry regions.
net.PSA_105_42: Yes, he remembered the sacred promise he made to Abraham his servant.
net.PSA_105_43: When he led his people out, they rejoiced; his chosen ones shouted with joy.
net.PSA_105_44: He handed the territory of nations over to them, and they took possession of what other peoples had produced,
net.PSA_105_45: so that they might keep his commands and obey his laws. Praise the Lord.
net.PSA_106_1: Praise the Lord. Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, and his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_106_2: Who can adequately recount the Lord’s mighty acts, or relate all his praiseworthy deeds?
net.PSA_106_3: How blessed are those who promote justice, and do what is right all the time.
net.PSA_106_4: Remember me, O Lord, when you show favor to your people. Pay attention to me, when you deliver,
net.PSA_106_5: so I may see the prosperity of your chosen ones, rejoice along with your nation, and boast along with the people who belong to you.
net.PSA_106_6: We have sinned like our ancestors; we have done wrong, we have done evil.
net.PSA_106_7: Our ancestors in Egypt failed to appreciate your miraculous deeds. They failed to remember your many acts of loyal love, and they rebelled at the sea, by the Red Sea.
net.PSA_106_8: Yet he delivered them for the sake of his reputation, that he might reveal his power.
net.PSA_106_9: He shouted at the Red Sea and it dried up; he led them through the deep water as if it were a desert.
net.PSA_106_10: He delivered them from the power of the one who hated them, and rescued them from the power of the enemy.
net.PSA_106_11: The water covered their enemies; not even one of them survived.
net.PSA_106_12: They believed his promises; they sang praises to him.
net.PSA_106_13: They quickly forgot what he had done; they did not wait for his instructions.
net.PSA_106_14: In the wilderness they had an insatiable craving for meat; they challenged God in the wastelands.
net.PSA_106_15: He granted their request, then struck them with a disease.
net.PSA_106_16: In the camp they resented Moses, and Aaron, the Lord’s holy priest.
net.PSA_106_17: The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan; it engulfed the group led by Abiram.
net.PSA_106_18: Fire burned their group; the flames scorched the wicked.
net.PSA_106_19: They made an image of a calf at Horeb, and worshiped a metal idol.
net.PSA_106_20: They traded their majestic God for the image of an ox that eats grass.
net.PSA_106_21: They rejected the God who delivered them, the one who performed great deeds in Egypt,
net.PSA_106_22: amazing feats in the land of Ham, mighty acts by the Red Sea.
net.PSA_106_23: He threatened to destroy them, but Moses, his chosen one, interceded with him and turned back his destructive anger.
net.PSA_106_24: They rejected the fruitful land; they did not believe his promise.
net.PSA_106_25: They grumbled in their tents; they did not obey the Lord.
net.PSA_106_26: So he made a solemn vow that he would make them die in the wilderness,
net.PSA_106_27: make their descendants die among the nations, and scatter them among foreign lands.
net.PSA_106_28: They worshiped Baal of Peor, and ate sacrifices offered to the dead.
net.PSA_106_29: They made the Lord angry by their actions, and a plague broke out among them.
net.PSA_106_30: Phinehas took a stand and intervened, and the plague subsided.
net.PSA_106_31: This was credited to Phinehas as a righteous act for all generations to come.
net.PSA_106_32: They made him angry by the waters of Meribah, and Moses suffered because of them,
net.PSA_106_33: for they aroused his temper, and he spoke rashly.
net.PSA_106_34: They did not destroy the nations, as the Lord had commanded them to do.
net.PSA_106_35: They mixed in with the nations and learned their ways.
net.PSA_106_36: They worshiped their idols, which became a snare to them.
net.PSA_106_37: They sacrificed their sons and daughters to demons.
net.PSA_106_38: They shed innocent blood— the blood of their sons and daughters, whom they sacrificed to the idols of Canaan. The land was polluted by bloodshed.
net.PSA_106_39: They were defiled by their deeds, and unfaithful in their actions.
net.PSA_106_40: So the Lord was angry with his people and despised the people who belonged to him.
net.PSA_106_41: He handed them over to the nations, and those who hated them ruled over them.
net.PSA_106_42: Their enemies oppressed them; they were subject to their authority.
net.PSA_106_43: Many times he delivered them, but they had a rebellious attitude, and degraded themselves by their sin.
net.PSA_106_44: Yet he took notice of their distress, when he heard their cry for help.
net.PSA_106_45: He remembered his covenant with them, and relented because of his great loyal love.
net.PSA_106_46: He caused all their conquerors to have pity on them.
net.PSA_106_47: Deliver us, O Lord, our God. Gather us from among the nations. Then we will give thanks to your holy name, and boast about your praiseworthy deeds.
net.PSA_106_48: The Lord God of Israel deserves praise, in the future and forevermore. Let all the people say, “We agree! Praise the Lord!”
net.PSA_107_1: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, and his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_107_2: Let those delivered by the Lord speak out, those whom he delivered from the power of the enemy,
net.PSA_107_3: and gathered from foreign lands, from east and west, from north and south.
net.PSA_107_4: They wandered through the wilderness, in a wasteland; they found no road to a city in which to live.
net.PSA_107_5: They were hungry and thirsty; they fainted from exhaustion.
net.PSA_107_6: They cried out to the Lord in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
net.PSA_107_7: He led them on a level road, that they might find a city in which to live.
net.PSA_107_8: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people.
net.PSA_107_9: For he has satisfied those who thirst, and those who hunger he has filled with food.
net.PSA_107_10: They sat in utter darkness, bound in painful iron chains,
net.PSA_107_11: because they had rebelled against God’s commands, and rejected the instructions of the Most High.
net.PSA_107_12: So he used suffering to humble them; they stumbled and no one helped them up.
net.PSA_107_13: They cried out to the Lord in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
net.PSA_107_14: He brought them out of the utter darkness, and tore off their shackles.
net.PSA_107_15: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people.
net.PSA_107_16: For he shattered the bronze gates, and hacked through the iron bars.
net.PSA_107_17: They acted like fools in their rebellious ways, and suffered because of their sins.
net.PSA_107_18: They lost their appetite for all food, and they drew near the gates of death.
net.PSA_107_19: They cried out to the Lord in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
net.PSA_107_20: He sent them an assuring word and healed them; he rescued them from the pits where they were trapped.
net.PSA_107_21: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people.
net.PSA_107_22: Let them present thank offerings, and loudly proclaim what he has done.
net.PSA_107_23: Some traveled on the sea in ships, and carried cargo over the vast waters.
net.PSA_107_24: They witnessed the acts of the Lord, his amazing feats on the deep water.
net.PSA_107_25: He gave the order for a windstorm, and it stirred up the waves of the sea.
net.PSA_107_26: They reached up to the sky, then dropped into the depths. The sailors’ strength left them because the danger was so great.
net.PSA_107_27: They swayed and staggered like drunks, and all their skill proved ineffective.
net.PSA_107_28: They cried out to the Lord in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
net.PSA_107_29: He calmed the storm, and the waves grew silent.
net.PSA_107_30: The sailors rejoiced because the waves grew quiet, and he led them to the harbor they desired.
net.PSA_107_31: Let them give thanks to the Lord for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people.
net.PSA_107_32: Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people. Let them praise him in the place where the leaders preside.
net.PSA_107_33: He turned streams into a desert, springs of water into arid land,
net.PSA_107_34: and a fruitful land into a barren place, because of the sin of its inhabitants.
net.PSA_107_35: As for his people, he turned a desert into a pool of water, and a dry land into springs of water.
net.PSA_107_36: He allowed the hungry to settle there, and they established a city in which to live.
net.PSA_107_37: They cultivated fields, and planted vineyards, which yielded a harvest of fruit.
net.PSA_107_38: He blessed them so that they became very numerous. He would not allow their cattle to decrease in number.
net.PSA_107_39: As for their enemies, they decreased in number and were beaten down, because of painful distress and suffering.
net.PSA_107_40: He would pour contempt upon princes, and he made them wander in a wasteland with no road.
net.PSA_107_41: Yet he protected the needy from oppression, and cared for his families like a flock of sheep.
net.PSA_107_42: When the godly see this, they rejoice, and every sinner shuts his mouth.
net.PSA_107_43: Whoever is wise, let him take note of these things. Let them consider the Lord’s acts of loyal love.
net.PSA_108_1: I am determined, O God. I will sing and praise you with my whole heart.
net.PSA_108_2: Awake, O stringed instrument and harp. I will wake up at dawn.
net.PSA_108_3: I will give you thanks before the nations, O Lord. I will sing praises to you before foreigners.
net.PSA_108_4: For your loyal love extends beyond the sky, and your faithfulness reaches the clouds.
net.PSA_108_5: Rise up above the sky, O God. May your splendor cover the whole earth.
net.PSA_108_6: Deliver by your power and answer me, so that the ones you love may be safe.
net.PSA_108_7: God has spoken in his sanctuary: “I will triumph! I will parcel out Shechem; the Valley of Sukkoth I will measure off.
net.PSA_108_8: Gilead belongs to me, as does Manasseh. Ephraim is my helmet, Judah my royal scepter.
net.PSA_108_9: Moab is my washbasin. I will make Edom serve me. I will shout in triumph over Philistia.”
net.PSA_108_10: Who will lead me into the fortified city? Who will bring me to Edom?
net.PSA_108_11: Have you not rejected us, O God? O God, you do not go into battle with our armies.
net.PSA_108_12: Give us help against the enemy, for any help men might offer is futile.
net.PSA_108_13: By God’s power we will conquer; he will trample down our enemies.
net.PSA_109_1: O God whom I praise, do not ignore me.
net.PSA_109_2: For they say cruel and deceptive things to me; they lie to me.
net.PSA_109_3: They surround me and say hateful things; they attack me for no reason.
net.PSA_109_4: They repay my love with accusations, but I continue to pray.
net.PSA_109_5: They repay me evil for good, and hate for love.
net.PSA_109_6: Appoint an evil man to testify against him. May an accuser stand at his right side.
net.PSA_109_7: When he is judged, he will be found guilty. Then his prayer will be regarded as sinful.
net.PSA_109_8: May his days be few. May another take his job.
net.PSA_109_9: May his children be fatherless, and his wife a widow.
net.PSA_109_10: May his children roam around begging, asking for handouts as they leave their ruined home.
net.PSA_109_11: May the creditor seize all he owns. May strangers loot his property.
net.PSA_109_12: May no one show him kindness. May no one have compassion on his fatherless children.
net.PSA_109_13: May his descendants be cut off. May the memory of them be wiped out by the time the next generation arrives.
net.PSA_109_14: May his ancestors’ sins be remembered by the Lord. May his mother’s sin not be forgotten.
net.PSA_109_15: May the Lord be constantly aware of them, and cut off the memory of his children from the earth.
net.PSA_109_16: For he never bothered to show kindness; he harassed the oppressed and needy, and killed the disheartened.
net.PSA_109_17: He loved to curse others, so those curses have come upon him. He had no desire to bless anyone, so he has experienced no blessings.
net.PSA_109_18: He made cursing a way of life, so curses poured into his stomach like water and seeped into his bones like oil.
net.PSA_109_19: May a curse attach itself to him, like a garment one puts on, or a belt one wears continually.
net.PSA_109_20: May the Lord repay my accusers in this way, those who say evil things about me.
net.PSA_109_21: O Sovereign Lord, intervene on my behalf for the sake of your reputation. Because your loyal love is good, deliver me.
net.PSA_109_22: For I am oppressed and needy, and my heart beats violently within me.
net.PSA_109_23: I am fading away like a shadow at the end of the day; I am shaken off like a locust.
net.PSA_109_24: I am so starved my knees shake; I have turned into skin and bones.
net.PSA_109_25: I am disdained by them. When they see me, they shake their heads.
net.PSA_109_26: Help me, O Lord my God. Because you are faithful to me, deliver me.
net.PSA_109_27: Then they will realize this is your work, and that you, Lord, have accomplished it.
net.PSA_109_28: They curse, but you will bless. When they attack, they will be humiliated, but your servant will rejoice.
net.PSA_109_29: My accusers will be covered with shame, and draped in humiliation as if it were a robe.
net.PSA_109_30: I will thank the Lord profusely. In the middle of a crowd I will praise him,
net.PSA_109_31: because he stands at the right hand of the needy, to deliver him from those who threaten his life.
net.PSA_110_1: Here is the Lord’s proclamation to my lord: “Sit down at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool.”
net.PSA_110_2: The Lord extends your dominion from Zion. Rule in the midst of your enemies.
net.PSA_110_3: Your people willingly follow you when you go into battle. On the holy hills at sunrise the dew of your youth belongs to you.
net.PSA_110_4: The Lord makes this promise on oath and will not revoke it: “You are an eternal priest after the pattern of Melchizedek.”
net.PSA_110_5: O Lord, at your right hand he strikes down kings in the day he unleashes his anger.
net.PSA_110_6: He executes judgment against the nations. He fills the valleys with corpses; he shatters their heads over the vast battlefield.
net.PSA_110_7: From the stream along the road he drinks; then he lifts up his head.
net.PSA_111_1: Praise the Lord! I will give thanks to the Lord with my whole heart, in the assembly of the godly and the congregation.
net.PSA_111_2: The Lord’s deeds are great, eagerly awaited by all who desire them.
net.PSA_111_3: His work is majestic and glorious, and his faithfulness endures forever.
net.PSA_111_4: He does amazing things that will be remembered; the Lord is merciful and compassionate.
net.PSA_111_5: He gives food to his faithful followers; he always remembers his covenant.
net.PSA_111_6: He announced that he would do mighty deeds for his people, giving them a land that belonged to other nations.
net.PSA_111_7: His acts are characterized by faithfulness and justice; all his precepts are reliable.
net.PSA_111_8: They are forever firm, and should be faithfully and properly carried out.
net.PSA_111_9: He delivered his people; he ordained that his covenant be observed forever. His name is holy and awesome.
net.PSA_111_10: To obey the Lord is the fundamental principle for wise living; all who carry out his precepts acquire good moral insight. He will receive praise forever.
net.PSA_112_1: Praise the Lord! How blessed is the one who obeys the Lord, who takes great delight in keeping his commands.
net.PSA_112_2: His descendants will be powerful on the earth; the godly will be blessed.
net.PSA_112_3: His house contains wealth and riches; his integrity endures.
net.PSA_112_4: In the darkness a light shines for the godly, for each one who is merciful, compassionate, and just.
net.PSA_112_5: It goes well for the one who generously lends money, and conducts his business honestly.
net.PSA_112_6: For he will never be shaken; others will always remember one who is just.
net.PSA_112_7: He does not fear bad news. He is confident; he trusts in the Lord.
net.PSA_112_8: His resolve is firm; he will not succumb to fear before he looks in triumph on his enemies.
net.PSA_112_9: He generously gives to the needy; his integrity endures. He will be vindicated and honored.
net.PSA_112_10: When the wicked see this, they will worry; they will grind their teeth in frustration and melt away. The desire of the wicked will perish.
net.PSA_113_1: Praise the Lord. Praise, you servants of the Lord, praise the name of the Lord.
net.PSA_113_2: May the Lord’s name be praised now and forevermore.
net.PSA_113_3: From east to west the Lord’s name is deserving of praise.
net.PSA_113_4: The Lord is exalted over all the nations; his splendor reaches beyond the sky.
net.PSA_113_5: Who can compare to the Lord our God, who sits on a high throne?
net.PSA_113_6: He bends down to look at the sky and the earth.
net.PSA_113_7: He raises the poor from the dirt, and lifts up the needy from the garbage pile,
net.PSA_113_8: that he might seat him with princes, with the princes of his people.
net.PSA_113_9: He makes the barren woman of the family a happy mother of children. Praise the Lord.
net.PSA_114_1: When Israel left Egypt, when the family of Jacob left a foreign nation behind,
net.PSA_114_2: Judah became his sanctuary, Israel his kingdom.
net.PSA_114_3: The sea looked and fled; the Jordan River turned back.
net.PSA_114_4: The mountains skipped like rams, the hills like lambs.
net.PSA_114_5: Why do you flee, O sea? Why do you turn back, O Jordan River?
net.PSA_114_6: Why do you skip like rams, O mountains, like lambs, O hills?
net.PSA_114_7: Tremble, O earth, before the Lord— before the God of Jacob,
net.PSA_114_8: who turned a rock into a pool of water, a hard rock into springs of water.
net.PSA_115_1: Not to us, O Lord, not to us, but to your name bring honor, for the sake of your loyal love and faithfulness.
net.PSA_115_2: Why should the nations say, “Where is their God?”
net.PSA_115_3: Our God is in heaven. He does whatever he pleases.
net.PSA_115_4: Their idols are made of silver and gold— they are man-made.
net.PSA_115_5: They have mouths, but cannot speak, eyes, but cannot see,
net.PSA_115_6: ears, but cannot hear, noses, but cannot smell,
net.PSA_115_7: hands, but cannot touch, feet, but cannot walk. They cannot even clear their throats.
net.PSA_115_8: Those who make them will end up like them, as will everyone who trusts in them.
net.PSA_115_9: O Israel, trust in the Lord. He is their deliverer and protector.
net.PSA_115_10: O family of Aaron, trust in the Lord. He is their deliverer and protector.
net.PSA_115_11: You loyal followers of the Lord, trust in the Lord. He is their deliverer and protector.
net.PSA_115_12: The Lord takes notice of us; he will bless — he will bless the family of Israel, he will bless the family of Aaron.
net.PSA_115_13: He will bless his loyal followers, both young and old.
net.PSA_115_14: May he increase your numbers, yours and your children’s.
net.PSA_115_15: May you be blessed by the Lord, the Creator of heaven and earth.
net.PSA_115_16: The heavens belong to the Lord, but the earth he has given to mankind.
net.PSA_115_17: The dead do not praise the Lord, nor do any of those who descend into the silence of death.
net.PSA_115_18: But we will praise the Lord now and forevermore. Praise the Lord!
net.PSA_116_1: I love the Lord because he heard my plea for mercy,
net.PSA_116_2: and listened to me. As long as I live, I will call to him when I need help.
net.PSA_116_3: The ropes of death tightened around me, the snares of Sheol confronted me. I was confronted with trouble and sorrow.
net.PSA_116_4: I called on the name of the Lord, “Please, Lord, rescue my life!”
net.PSA_116_5: The Lord is merciful and fair; our God is compassionate.
net.PSA_116_6: The Lord protects the untrained; I was in serious trouble and he delivered me.
net.PSA_116_7: Rest once more, my soul, for the Lord has vindicated you.
net.PSA_116_8: Yes, Lord, you rescued my life from death, kept my eyes from tears and my feet from stumbling.
net.PSA_116_9: I will serve the Lord in the land of the living.
net.PSA_116_10: I had faith when I said, “I am severely oppressed.”
net.PSA_116_11: I rashly declared, “All men are liars.”
net.PSA_116_12: How can I repay the Lord for all his acts of kindness to me?
net.PSA_116_13: I will celebrate my deliverance, and call on the name of the Lord.
net.PSA_116_14: I will fulfill my vows to the Lord before all his people.
net.PSA_116_15: The Lord values the lives of his faithful followers.
net.PSA_116_16: Yes, Lord! I am indeed your servant; I am your servant, the son of your female servant. You saved me from death.
net.PSA_116_17: I will present a thank offering to you, and call on the name of the Lord.
net.PSA_116_18: I will fulfill my vows to the Lord before all his people,
net.PSA_116_19: in the courts of the Lord’s temple, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the Lord!
net.PSA_117_1: Praise the Lord, all you nations. Applaud him, all you foreigners.
net.PSA_117_2: For his loyal love towers over us, and the Lord’s faithfulness endures. Praise the Lord.
net.PSA_118_1: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, and his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_118_2: Let Israel say, “Yes, his loyal love endures.”
net.PSA_118_3: Let the family of Aaron say, “Yes, his loyal love endures.”
net.PSA_118_4: Let the loyal followers of the Lord say, “Yes, his loyal love endures.”
net.PSA_118_5: In my distress I cried out to the Lord. The Lord answered me and put me in a wide open place.
net.PSA_118_6: The Lord is on my side; I am not afraid. What can people do to me?
net.PSA_118_7: The Lord is on my side as my helper. I look in triumph on those who hate me.
net.PSA_118_8: It is better to take shelter in the Lord than to trust in people.
net.PSA_118_9: It is better to take shelter in the Lord than to trust in princes.
net.PSA_118_10: All the nations surrounded me. Indeed, in the name of the Lord I pushed them away.
net.PSA_118_11: They surrounded me, yes, they surrounded me. Indeed, in the name of the Lord I pushed them away.
net.PSA_118_12: They surrounded me like bees. But they disappeared as quickly as a fire among thorns. Indeed, in the name of the Lord I pushed them away.
net.PSA_118_13: “You aggressively attacked me and tried to knock me down, but the Lord helped me.
net.PSA_118_14: The Lord gives me strength and protects me; he has become my deliverer.”
net.PSA_118_15: They celebrate deliverance in the tents of the godly. The Lord’s right hand conquers.
net.PSA_118_16: The Lord’s right hand gives victory; the Lord’s right hand conquers.
net.PSA_118_17: I will not die, but live, and I will proclaim what the Lord has done.
net.PSA_118_18: The Lord severely punished me, but he did not hand me over to death.
net.PSA_118_19: Open for me the gates of the just king’s temple. I will enter through them and give thanks to the Lord.
net.PSA_118_20: This is the Lord’s gate— the godly enter through it.
net.PSA_118_21: I will give you thanks, for you answered me, and have become my deliverer.
net.PSA_118_22: The stone that the builders discarded has become the cornerstone.
net.PSA_118_23: This is the Lord’s work. We consider it amazing!
net.PSA_118_24: This is the day the Lord has brought about. We will be happy and rejoice in it.
net.PSA_118_25: Please, Lord, deliver! Please, Lord, grant us success!
net.PSA_118_26: May the one who comes in the name of the Lord be blessed. We will pronounce blessings on you in the Lord’s temple.
net.PSA_118_27: The Lord is God, and he has delivered us. Tie the offering with ropes to the horns of the altar.
net.PSA_118_28: You are my God, and I will give you thanks. You are my God and I will praise you.
net.PSA_118_29: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good and his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_119_1: How blessed are those whose actions are blameless, who obey the law of the Lord.
net.PSA_119_2: How blessed are those who observe his rules, and seek him with all their heart,
net.PSA_119_3: who, moreover, do no wrong, but follow in his footsteps.
net.PSA_119_4: You demand that your precepts be carefully kept.
net.PSA_119_5: If only I were predisposed to keep your statutes.
net.PSA_119_6: Then I would not be ashamed, if I were focused on all your commands.
net.PSA_119_7: I will give you sincere thanks, when I learn your just regulations.
net.PSA_119_8: I will keep your statutes. Do not completely abandon me. ב (Bet)
net.PSA_119_9: How can a young person maintain a pure life? By guarding it according to your instructions.
net.PSA_119_10: With all my heart I seek you. Do not allow me to stray from your commands.
net.PSA_119_11: In my heart I store up your words, so I might not sin against you.
net.PSA_119_12: You deserve praise, O Lord. Teach me your statutes.
net.PSA_119_13: With my lips I proclaim all the regulations you have revealed.
net.PSA_119_14: I rejoice in the lifestyle prescribed by your rules as if they were riches of all kinds.
net.PSA_119_15: I will meditate on your precepts and focus on your behavior.
net.PSA_119_16: I find delight in your statutes; I do not forget your instructions. ג (Gimel)
net.PSA_119_17: Be kind to your servant. Then I will live and keep your instructions.
net.PSA_119_18: Open my eyes so I can truly see the marvelous things in your law.
net.PSA_119_19: I am a resident foreigner in this land. Do not hide your commands from me.
net.PSA_119_20: I desperately long to know your regulations at all times.
net.PSA_119_21: You reprimand arrogant people. Those who stray from your commands are doomed.
net.PSA_119_22: Spare me shame and humiliation, for I observe your rules.
net.PSA_119_23: Though rulers plot and slander me, your servant meditates on your statutes.
net.PSA_119_24: Yes, I find delight in your rules; they give me guidance. ד (Dalet)
net.PSA_119_25: I collapse in the dirt. Revive me with your word.
net.PSA_119_26: I told you about my ways and you answered me. Teach me your statutes.
net.PSA_119_27: Help me to understand what your precepts mean. Then I can meditate on your marvelous teachings.
net.PSA_119_28: I collapse from grief. Sustain me by your word.
net.PSA_119_29: Remove me from the path of deceit. Graciously give me your law.
net.PSA_119_30: I choose the path of faithfulness; I am committed to your regulations.
net.PSA_119_31: I hold fast to your rules. O Lord, do not let me be ashamed.
net.PSA_119_32: I run along the path of your commands, for you enable me to do so. ה (He)
net.PSA_119_33: Teach me, O Lord, the lifestyle prescribed by your statutes, so that I might observe it continually.
net.PSA_119_34: Give me understanding so that I might observe your law, and keep it with all my heart.
net.PSA_119_35: Guide me in the path of your commands, for I delight to walk in it.
net.PSA_119_36: Give me a desire for your rules, rather than for wealth gained unjustly.
net.PSA_119_37: Turn my eyes away from what is worthless. Revive me with your word.
net.PSA_119_38: Confirm to your servant your promise, which you made to the one who honors you.
net.PSA_119_39: Take away the insults that I dread. Indeed, your regulations are good.
net.PSA_119_40: Look, I long for your precepts. Revive me with your deliverance. ו (Vav)
net.PSA_119_41: May I experience your loyal love, O Lord, and your deliverance, as you promised.
net.PSA_119_42: Then I will have a reply for the one who insults me, for I trust in your word.
net.PSA_119_43: Do not completely deprive me of a truthful testimony, for I await your justice.
net.PSA_119_44: Then I will keep your law continually now and for all time.
net.PSA_119_45: I will be secure, for I seek your precepts.
net.PSA_119_46: I will speak about your regulations before kings and not be ashamed.
net.PSA_119_47: I will find delight in your commands, which I love.
net.PSA_119_48: I will lift my hands to your commands, which I love, and I will meditate on your statutes. ז (Zayin)
net.PSA_119_49: Remember your word to your servant, for you have given me hope.
net.PSA_119_50: This is what comforts me in my trouble, for your promise revives me.
net.PSA_119_51: Arrogant people do nothing but scoff at me. Yet I do not turn aside from your law.
net.PSA_119_52: I remember your ancient regulations, O Lord, and console myself.
net.PSA_119_53: Rage takes hold of me because of the wicked, those who reject your law.
net.PSA_119_54: Your statutes have been my songs in the house where I live.
net.PSA_119_55: I remember your name during the night, O Lord, and I will keep your law.
net.PSA_119_56: This has been my practice, for I observe your precepts. ח (Khet)
net.PSA_119_57: The Lord is my source of security. I have determined to follow your instructions.
net.PSA_119_58: I seek your favor with all my heart. Have mercy on me as you promised.
net.PSA_119_59: I consider my actions and follow your rules.
net.PSA_119_60: I keep your commands eagerly and without delay.
net.PSA_119_61: The ropes of the wicked tighten around me, but I do not forget your law.
net.PSA_119_62: In the middle of the night I arise to thank you for your just regulations.
net.PSA_119_63: I am a friend to all your loyal followers, and to those who keep your precepts.
net.PSA_119_64: O Lord, your loyal love fills the earth. Teach me your statutes! ט (Tet)
net.PSA_119_65: You are good to your servant, O Lord, just as you promised.
net.PSA_119_66: Teach me proper discernment and understanding. For I consider your commands to be reliable.
net.PSA_119_67: Before I was afflicted I used to stray off, but now I keep your instructions.
net.PSA_119_68: You are good and you do good. Teach me your statutes.
net.PSA_119_69: Arrogant people smear my reputation with lies, but I observe your precepts with all my heart.
net.PSA_119_70: Their hearts are calloused, but I find delight in your law.
net.PSA_119_71: It was good for me to suffer, so that I might learn your statutes.
net.PSA_119_72: The law you have revealed is more important to me than thousands of pieces of gold and silver. י (Yod)
net.PSA_119_73: Your hands made me and formed me. Give me understanding so that I might learn your commands.
net.PSA_119_74: Your loyal followers will be glad when they see me, for I find hope in your word.
net.PSA_119_75: I know, Lord, that your regulations are just. You disciplined me because of your faithful devotion to me.
net.PSA_119_76: May your loyal love console me, as you promised your servant.
net.PSA_119_77: May I experience your compassion, so I might live. For I find delight in your law.
net.PSA_119_78: May the arrogant be humiliated, for they have slandered me. But I meditate on your precepts.
net.PSA_119_79: May your loyal followers turn to me, those who know your rules.
net.PSA_119_80: May I be fully committed to your statutes, so that I might not be ashamed. כ (Kaf)
net.PSA_119_81: I desperately long for your deliverance. I find hope in your word.
net.PSA_119_82: My eyes grow tired as I wait for your promise to be fulfilled. I say, “When will you comfort me?”
net.PSA_119_83: For I am like a wineskin dried up in smoke. I do not forget your statutes.
net.PSA_119_84: How long must your servant endure this? When will you judge those who pursue me?
net.PSA_119_85: The arrogant dig pits to trap me, which violates your law.
net.PSA_119_86: All your commands are reliable. I am pursued without reason. Help me!
net.PSA_119_87: They have almost destroyed me here on the earth, but I do not reject your precepts.
net.PSA_119_88: Revive me with your loyal love, that I might keep the rules you have revealed. ל (Lamed)
net.PSA_119_89: O Lord, your instructions endure; they stand secure in heaven.
net.PSA_119_90: You demonstrate your faithfulness to all generations. You established the earth and it stood firm.
net.PSA_119_91: Today they stand firm by your decrees, for all things are your servants.
net.PSA_119_92: If I had not found encouragement in your law, I would have died in my sorrow.
net.PSA_119_93: I will never forget your precepts, for by them you have revived me.
net.PSA_119_94: I belong to you. Deliver me! For I seek your precepts.
net.PSA_119_95: The wicked prepare to kill me, yet I concentrate on your rules.
net.PSA_119_96: I realize that everything has its limits, but your commands are beyond full comprehension. מ (Mem)
net.PSA_119_97: O how I love your law! All day long I meditate on it.
net.PSA_119_98: Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies, for I am always aware of them.
net.PSA_119_99: I have more insight than all my teachers, for I meditate on your rules.
net.PSA_119_100: I am more discerning than those older than I, for I observe your precepts.
net.PSA_119_101: I stay away from every evil path, so that I might keep your instructions.
net.PSA_119_102: I do not turn aside from your regulations, for you teach me.
net.PSA_119_103: Your words are sweeter in my mouth than honey!
net.PSA_119_104: Your precepts give me discernment. Therefore I hate all deceitful actions. נ (Nun)
net.PSA_119_105: Your word is a lamp to walk by, and a light to illumine my path.
net.PSA_119_106: I have vowed and solemnly sworn to keep your just regulations.
net.PSA_119_107: I am suffering terribly. O Lord, revive me with your word.
net.PSA_119_108: O Lord, please accept the freewill offerings of my praise. Teach me your regulations.
net.PSA_119_109: My life is in continual danger, but I do not forget your law.
net.PSA_119_110: The wicked lay a trap for me, but I do not wander from your precepts.
net.PSA_119_111: I claim your rules as my permanent possession, for they give me joy.
net.PSA_119_112: I am determined to obey your statutes at all times, to the very end. ס (Samek)
net.PSA_119_113: I hate people with divided loyalties, but I love your law.
net.PSA_119_114: You are my hiding place and my shield. I find hope in your word.
net.PSA_119_115: Turn away from me, you evil men, so that I can observe the commands of my God.
net.PSA_119_116: Sustain me as you promised, so that I will live. Do not disappoint me.
net.PSA_119_117: Support me, so that I will be delivered. Then I will focus on your statutes continually.
net.PSA_119_118: You despise all who stray from your statutes, for such people are deceptive and unreliable.
net.PSA_119_119: You remove all the wicked of the earth like slag. Therefore I love your rules.
net.PSA_119_120: My body trembles because I fear you; I am afraid of your judgments. ע (Ayin)
net.PSA_119_121: I do what is fair and right. Do not abandon me to my oppressors.
net.PSA_119_122: Guarantee the welfare of your servant. Do not let the arrogant oppress me.
net.PSA_119_123: My eyes grow tired as I wait for your deliverance, for your reliable promise to be fulfilled.
net.PSA_119_124: Show your servant your loyal love. Teach me your statutes.
net.PSA_119_125: I am your servant. Give me insight, so that I can understand your rules.
net.PSA_119_126: It is time for the Lord to act— they break your law.
net.PSA_119_127: For this reason I love your commands more than gold, even purest gold.
net.PSA_119_128: For this reason I carefully follow all your precepts. I hate all deceitful actions. פ (Pe)
net.PSA_119_129: Your rules are marvelous. Therefore I observe them.
net.PSA_119_130: Your instructions are a doorway through which light shines. They give insight to the untrained.
net.PSA_119_131: I open my mouth and pant, because I long for your commands.
net.PSA_119_132: Turn toward me and extend mercy to me, as you typically do to your loyal followers.
net.PSA_119_133: Direct my steps by your word. Do not let any sin dominate me.
net.PSA_119_134: Deliver me from oppressive men, so that I can keep your precepts.
net.PSA_119_135: Smile on your servant. Teach me your statutes!
net.PSA_119_136: Tears stream down from my eyes, because people do not keep your law. צ (Tsade)
net.PSA_119_137: You are just, O Lord, and your judgments are fair.
net.PSA_119_138: The rules you impose are just, and absolutely reliable.
net.PSA_119_139: My zeal consumes me, for my enemies forget your instructions.
net.PSA_119_140: Your word is absolutely pure, and your servant loves it.
net.PSA_119_141: I am insignificant and despised, yet I do not forget your precepts.
net.PSA_119_142: Your justice endures, and your law is reliable.
net.PSA_119_143: Distress and hardship confront me, yet I find delight in your commands.
net.PSA_119_144: Your rules remain just. Give me insight so that I can live. ק (Qof)
net.PSA_119_145: I cried out with all my heart, “Answer me, O Lord! I will observe your statutes.”
net.PSA_119_146: I cried out to you, “Deliver me, so that I can keep your rules.”
net.PSA_119_147: I am up before dawn crying for help. I find hope in your word.
net.PSA_119_148: My eyes anticipate the nighttime hours, so that I can meditate on your word.
net.PSA_119_149: Listen to me because of your loyal love. O Lord, revive me, as you typically do.
net.PSA_119_150: Those who are eager to do wrong draw near; they are far from your law.
net.PSA_119_151: You are near, O Lord, and all your commands are reliable.
net.PSA_119_152: I learned long ago that you ordained your rules to last. ר (Resh)
net.PSA_119_153: See my pain and rescue me. For I do not forget your law.
net.PSA_119_154: Fight for me and defend me. Revive me with your word.
net.PSA_119_155: The wicked have no chance for deliverance, for they do not seek your statutes.
net.PSA_119_156: Your compassion is great, O Lord. Revive me, as you typically do.
net.PSA_119_157: The enemies who chase me are numerous. Yet I do not turn aside from your rules.
net.PSA_119_158: I take note of the treacherous and despise them, because they do not keep your instructions.
net.PSA_119_159: See how I love your precepts. O Lord, revive me with your loyal love.
net.PSA_119_160: Your instructions are totally reliable; all your just regulations endure. שׂ/שׁ (Sin/Shin)
net.PSA_119_161: Rulers pursue me for no reason, yet I am more afraid of disobeying your instructions.
net.PSA_119_162: I rejoice in your instructions, like one who finds much plunder.
net.PSA_119_163: I hate and despise deceit; I love your law.
net.PSA_119_164: Seven times a day I praise you because of your just regulations.
net.PSA_119_165: Those who love your law are completely secure; nothing causes them to stumble.
net.PSA_119_166: I hope for your deliverance, O Lord, and I obey your commands.
net.PSA_119_167: I keep your rules; I love them greatly.
net.PSA_119_168: I keep your precepts and rules, for you are aware of everything I do. ת (Tav)
net.PSA_119_169: Listen to my cry for help, O Lord. Give me insight by your word.
net.PSA_119_170: Listen to my appeal for mercy. Deliver me, as you promised.
net.PSA_119_171: May praise flow freely from my lips, for you teach me your statutes.
net.PSA_119_172: May my tongue sing about your instructions, for all your commands are just.
net.PSA_119_173: May your hand help me, for I choose to obey your precepts.
net.PSA_119_174: I long for your deliverance, O Lord; I find delight in your law.
net.PSA_119_175: May I live and praise you. May your regulations help me.
net.PSA_119_176: I have wandered off like a lost sheep. Come looking for your servant, for I do not forget your commands.
net.PSA_120_1: In my distress I cried out to the Lord and he answered me.
net.PSA_120_2: I said, “O Lord, rescue me from those who lie with their lips and those who deceive with their tongues.
net.PSA_120_3: How will he severely punish you, you deceptive talker?
net.PSA_120_4: Here’s how! With the sharp arrows of warriors, with arrowheads forged over the hot coals.
net.PSA_120_5: How miserable I am. For I have lived temporarily in Meshech; I have resided among the tents of Kedar.
net.PSA_120_6: For too long I have had to reside with those who hate peace.
net.PSA_120_7: I am committed to peace, but when I speak, they want to make war.
net.PSA_121_1: I look up toward the hills. From where does my help come?
net.PSA_121_2: My help comes from the Lord, the Creator of heaven and earth.
net.PSA_121_3: May he not allow your foot to slip. May your Protector not sleep.
net.PSA_121_4: Look! Israel’s Protector does not sleep or slumber.
net.PSA_121_5: The Lord is your protector; the Lord is the shade at your right hand.
net.PSA_121_6: The sun will not harm you by day, or the moon by night.
net.PSA_121_7: The Lord will protect you from all harm; he will protect your life.
net.PSA_121_8: The Lord will protect you in all you do, now and forevermore.
net.PSA_122_1: I was glad because they said to me, “We will go to the Lord’s temple.”
net.PSA_122_2: Our feet are standing inside your gates, O Jerusalem.
net.PSA_122_3: Jerusalem is a city designed to accommodate an assembly.
net.PSA_122_4: The tribes go up there, the tribes of the Lord, where it is required that Israel give thanks to the name of the Lord.
net.PSA_122_5: Indeed, the leaders sit there on thrones and make legal decisions, on the thrones of the house of David.
net.PSA_122_6: Pray for the peace of Jerusalem. May those who love her prosper.
net.PSA_122_7: May there be peace inside your defenses, and prosperity inside your fortresses.
net.PSA_122_8: For the sake of my brothers and my neighbors I will say, “May there be peace in you.”
net.PSA_122_9: For the sake of the temple of the Lord our God I will pray for you to prosper.
net.PSA_123_1: I look up toward you, the one enthroned in heaven.
net.PSA_123_2: Look, as the eyes of servants look to the hand of their master, as the eyes of a female servant look to the hand of her mistress, so our eyes will look to the Lord, our God, until he shows us favor.
net.PSA_123_3: Show us favor, O Lord, show us favor! For we have had our fill of humiliation, and then some.
net.PSA_123_4: We have had our fill of the taunts of the self-assured, of the contempt of the proud.
net.PSA_124_1: “If the Lord had not been on our side”— let Israel say this.—
net.PSA_124_2: if the Lord had not been on our side, when men attacked us,
net.PSA_124_3: they would have swallowed us alive, when their anger raged against us.
net.PSA_124_4: The water would have overpowered us; the current would have overwhelmed us.
net.PSA_124_5: The raging water would have overwhelmed us.
net.PSA_124_6: The Lord deserves praise, for he did not hand us over as prey to their teeth.
net.PSA_124_7: We escaped with our lives, like a bird from a hunter’s snare. The snare broke, and we escaped.
net.PSA_124_8: Our deliverer is the Lord, the Creator of heaven and earth.
net.PSA_125_1: Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion, which cannot be moved and will endure forever.
net.PSA_125_2: As the mountains surround Jerusalem, so the Lord surrounds his people, now and forevermore.
net.PSA_125_3: Indeed, the scepter of a wicked king will not settle upon the allotted land of the godly. Otherwise the godly might do what is wrong.
net.PSA_125_4: Do good, O Lord, to those who are good, to the morally upright.
net.PSA_125_5: As for those who are bent on traveling a sinful path, may the Lord remove them, along with those who behave wickedly. May Israel experience peace.
net.PSA_126_1: When the Lord restored the well-being of Zion, we thought we were dreaming.
net.PSA_126_2: At that time we laughed loudly and shouted for joy. At that time the nations said, “The Lord has accomplished great things for these people.”
net.PSA_126_3: The Lord did indeed accomplish great things for us. We were happy.
net.PSA_126_4: O Lord, restore our well-being, just as the streams in the arid south are replenished.
net.PSA_126_5: Those who shed tears as they plant will shout for joy when they reap the harvest.
net.PSA_126_6: The one who weeps as he walks along, carrying his bag of seed, will certainly come in with a shout of joy, carrying his sheaves of grain.
net.PSA_127_1: If the Lord does not build a house, then those who build it work in vain. If the Lord does not guard a city, then the watchman stands guard in vain.
net.PSA_127_2: It is vain for you to rise early, come home late, and work so hard for your food. Yes, he provides for those whom he loves even when they sleep.
net.PSA_127_3: Yes, sons are a gift from the Lord; the fruit of the womb is a reward.
net.PSA_127_4: Sons born during one’s youth are like arrows in a warrior’s hand.
net.PSA_127_5: How blessed is the man who fills his quiver with them. They will not be put to shame when they confront enemies at the city gate.
net.PSA_128_1: How blessed is every one of the Lord’s loyal followers, each one who keeps his commands.
net.PSA_128_2: You will eat what you worked so hard to grow. You will be blessed and secure.
net.PSA_128_3: Your wife will be like a fruitful vine in the inner rooms of your house; your children will be like olive branches, as they sit all around your table.
net.PSA_128_4: Yes indeed, the man who fears the Lord will be blessed in this way.
net.PSA_128_5: May the Lord bless you from Zion, that you might see Jerusalem prosper all the days of your life,
net.PSA_128_6: and that you might see your grandchildren. May Israel experience peace.
net.PSA_129_1: “Since my youth they have often attacked me,” let Israel say.
net.PSA_129_2: “Since my youth they have often attacked me, but they have not defeated me.
net.PSA_129_3: The plowers plowed my back; they made their furrows long.
net.PSA_129_4: The Lord is just; he cut the ropes of the wicked.”
net.PSA_129_5: May all who hate Zion be humiliated and turned back.
net.PSA_129_6: May they be like the grass on the rooftops, which withers before one can even pull it up,
net.PSA_129_7: which cannot fill the reaper’s hand, or the lap of the one who gathers the grain.
net.PSA_129_8: Those who pass by will not say, “May you experience the Lord’s blessing! We pronounce a blessing on you in the name of the Lord.”
net.PSA_130_1: From the deep water I cry out to you, O Lord.
net.PSA_130_2: O Lord, listen to me. Pay attention to my plea for mercy.
net.PSA_130_3: If you, O Lord, were to keep track of sins, O Lord, who could stand before you?
net.PSA_130_4: But you are willing to forgive, so that you might be honored.
net.PSA_130_5: I rely on the Lord. I rely on him with my whole being; I wait for his assuring word.
net.PSA_130_6: I yearn for the Lord, more than watchmen do for the morning, yes, more than watchmen do for the morning.
net.PSA_130_7: O Israel, hope in the Lord, for the Lord exhibits loyal love, and is more than willing to deliver.
net.PSA_130_8: He will deliver Israel from all their sins.
net.PSA_131_1: O Lord, my heart is not proud, nor do I have a haughty look. I do not have great aspirations, or concern myself with things that are beyond me.
net.PSA_131_2: Indeed, I have calmed and quieted myself like a weaned child with its mother; I am content like a young child.
net.PSA_131_3: O Israel, hope in the Lord now and forevermore!
net.PSA_132_1: O Lord, for David’s sake remember all his strenuous effort,
net.PSA_132_2: and how he made a vow to the Lord, and swore an oath to the Powerful One of Jacob.
net.PSA_132_3: He said, “I will not enter my own home, or get into my bed.
net.PSA_132_4: I will not allow my eyes to sleep, or my eyelids to slumber,
net.PSA_132_5: until I find a place for the Lord, a fine dwelling place for the Powerful One of Jacob.”
net.PSA_132_6: Look, we heard about it in Ephrathah; we found it in the territory of Jaar.
net.PSA_132_7: Let us go to his dwelling place. Let us worship before his footstool.
net.PSA_132_8: Ascend, O Lord, to your resting place, you and the ark of your strength.
net.PSA_132_9: May your priests be clothed with integrity. May your loyal followers shout for joy.
net.PSA_132_10: For the sake of David, your servant, do not reject your chosen king.
net.PSA_132_11: The Lord made a reliable promise to David; he will not go back on his word. He said, “I will place one of your descendants on your throne.
net.PSA_132_12: If your sons keep my covenant and the rules I teach them, their sons will also sit on your throne forever.”
net.PSA_132_13: Certainly the Lord has chosen Zion; he decided to make it his home.
net.PSA_132_14: He said, “This will be my resting place forever; I will live here, for I have chosen it.
net.PSA_132_15: I will abundantly supply what she needs; I will give her poor all the food they need.
net.PSA_132_16: I will protect her priests, and her godly people will shout exuberantly.
net.PSA_132_17: There I will make David strong; I have determined that my chosen king’s dynasty will continue.
net.PSA_132_18: I will humiliate his enemies, and his crown will shine.”
net.PSA_133_1: Look! How good and how pleasant it is when brothers truly live in unity.
net.PSA_133_2: It is like fine oil poured on the head, which flows down the beard — Aaron’s beard, and then flows down his garments.
net.PSA_133_3: It is like the dew of Hermon, which flows down upon the hills of Zion. Indeed, that is where the Lord has decreed a blessing will be available—eternal life.
net.PSA_134_1: Attention! Praise the Lord, all you servants of the Lord, who serve in the Lord’s temple during the night.
net.PSA_134_2: Lift your hands toward the sanctuary and praise the Lord.
net.PSA_134_3: May the Lord, the Creator of heaven and earth, bless you from Zion.
net.PSA_135_1: Praise the Lord. Praise the name of the Lord. Offer praise, you servants of the Lord,
net.PSA_135_2: who serve in the Lord’s temple, in the courts of the temple of our God.
net.PSA_135_3: Praise the Lord, for the Lord is good. Sing praises to his name, for it is pleasant.
net.PSA_135_4: Indeed, the Lord has chosen Jacob for himself, Israel to be his special possession.
net.PSA_135_5: Yes, I know the Lord is great, and our Lord is superior to all gods.
net.PSA_135_6: He does whatever he pleases in heaven and on earth, in the seas and all the ocean depths.
net.PSA_135_7: He causes the clouds to arise from the end of the earth, makes lightning bolts accompany the rain, and brings the wind out of his storehouses.
net.PSA_135_8: He struck down the firstborn of Egypt, including both men and animals.
net.PSA_135_9: He performed awesome deeds and acts of judgment in your midst, O Egypt, against Pharaoh and all his servants.
net.PSA_135_10: He defeated many nations, and killed mighty kings—
net.PSA_135_11: Sihon, king of the Amorites, and Og, king of Bashan, and all the kingdoms of Canaan.
net.PSA_135_12: He gave their land as an inheritance, as an inheritance to Israel his people.
net.PSA_135_13: O Lord, your name endures, your reputation, O Lord, lasts.
net.PSA_135_14: For the Lord vindicates his people, and has compassion on his servants.
net.PSA_135_15: The nations’ idols are made of silver and gold; they are man-made.
net.PSA_135_16: They have mouths, but cannot speak, eyes, but cannot see,
net.PSA_135_17: and ears, but cannot hear. Indeed, they cannot breathe.
net.PSA_135_18: Those who make them will end up like them, as will everyone who trusts in them.
net.PSA_135_19: O family of Israel, praise the Lord. O family of Aaron, praise the Lord.
net.PSA_135_20: O family of Levi, praise the Lord. You loyal followers of the Lord, praise the Lord.
net.PSA_135_21: The Lord deserves praise in Zion — he who dwells in Jerusalem. Praise the Lord.
net.PSA_136_1: Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good, for his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_136_2: Give thanks to the God of gods, for his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_136_3: Give thanks to the Lord of lords, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_4: to the one who performs magnificent, amazing deeds all by himself, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_5: to the one who used wisdom to make the heavens, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_6: to the one who spread out the earth over the water, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_7: to the one who made the great lights, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_8: the sun to rule by day, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_9: the moon and stars to rule by night, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_10: to the one who struck down the firstborn of Egypt, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_11: and led Israel out from their midst, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_12: with a strong hand and an outstretched arm, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_13: to the one who divided the Red Sea in two, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_14: and led Israel through its midst, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_15: and tossed Pharaoh and his army into the Red Sea, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_16: to the one who led his people through the wilderness, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_17: to the one who struck down great kings, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_18: and killed powerful kings, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_19: Sihon, king of the Amorites, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_20: Og, king of Bashan, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_21: and gave their land as an inheritance, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_22: as an inheritance to Israel his servant, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_23: to the one who remembered us when we were down, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_24: and snatched us away from our enemies, for his loyal love endures,
net.PSA_136_25: to the one who gives food to all living things, for his loyal love endures.
net.PSA_136_26: Give thanks to the God of heaven, for his loyal love endures!
net.PSA_137_1: By the rivers of Babylon we sit down and weep when we remember Zion.
net.PSA_137_2: On the poplars in her midst we hang our harps,
net.PSA_137_3: for there our captors ask us to compose songs; those who mock us demand that we be happy, saying: “Sing for us a song about Zion!”
net.PSA_137_4: How can we sing a song to the Lord in a foreign land?
net.PSA_137_5: If I forget you, O Jerusalem, may my right hand be crippled.
net.PSA_137_6: May my tongue stick to the roof of my mouth, if I do not remember you, and do not give Jerusalem priority over whatever gives me the most joy.
net.PSA_137_7: Remember, O Lord, what the Edomites did on the day Jerusalem fell. They said, “Tear it down, tear it down, right to its very foundation!”
net.PSA_137_8: O daughter Babylon, soon to be devastated, how blessed will be the one who repays you for what you dished out to us.
net.PSA_137_9: How blessed will be the one who grabs your babies and smashes them on a rock.
net.PSA_138_1: I will give you thanks with all my heart; before the heavenly assembly I will sing praises to you.
net.PSA_138_2: I will bow down toward your holy temple, and give thanks to your name, because of your loyal love and faithfulness, for you have exalted your promise above the entire sky.
net.PSA_138_3: When I cried out for help, you answered me. You made me bold and energized me.
net.PSA_138_4: Let all the kings of the earth give thanks to you, O Lord, when they hear the words you speak.
net.PSA_138_5: Let them sing about the Lord’s deeds, for the Lord’s splendor is magnificent.
net.PSA_138_6: Though the Lord is exalted, he looks after the lowly, and from far away humbles the proud.
net.PSA_138_7: Even when I must walk in the midst of danger, you revive me. You oppose my angry enemies, and your right hand delivers me.
net.PSA_138_8: The Lord avenges me. O Lord, your loyal love endures. Do not abandon those whom you have made.
net.PSA_139_1: O Lord, you examine me and know me.
net.PSA_139_2: You know when I sit down and when I get up; even from far away you understand my motives.
net.PSA_139_3: You carefully observe me when I travel or when I lie down to rest; you are aware of everything I do.
net.PSA_139_4: Certainly my tongue does not frame a word without you, O Lord, being thoroughly aware of it.
net.PSA_139_5: You squeeze me in from behind and in front; you place your hand on me.
net.PSA_139_6: Your knowledge is beyond my comprehension; it is so far beyond me, I am unable to fathom it.
net.PSA_139_7: Where can I go to escape your Spirit? Where can I flee to escape your presence?
net.PSA_139_8: If I were to ascend to heaven, you would be there. If I were to sprawl out in Sheol, there you would be.
net.PSA_139_9: If I were to fly away on the wings of the dawn, and settle down on the other side of the sea,
net.PSA_139_10: even there your hand would guide me, your right hand would grab hold of me.
net.PSA_139_11: If I were to say, “Certainly the darkness will cover me, and the light will turn to night all around me,”
net.PSA_139_12: even the darkness is not too dark for you to see, and the night is as bright as day; darkness and light are the same to you.
net.PSA_139_13: Certainly you made my mind and heart; you wove me together in my mother’s womb.
net.PSA_139_14: I will give you thanks because your deeds are awesome and amazing. You knew me thoroughly;
net.PSA_139_15: my bones were not hidden from you, when I was made in secret and sewed together in the depths of the earth.
net.PSA_139_16: Your eyes saw me when I was inside the womb. All the days ordained for me were recorded in your scroll before one of them came into existence.
net.PSA_139_17: How difficult it is for me to fathom your thoughts about me, O God! How vast is their sum total.
